title: Symbolic Layers — Animal → Geometry → Physics → Ecology → Generational → Energy Law

heading_structure.title: Symbolic Layers — Animal → Geometry → Physics → Ecology → Generational → Energy Law

code_blocks:
import json
from src.score import aggregate

# General sensor evaluation pattern
sensor = {
    "id": "general_assessment",
    "signals": [
        {"name": "primary_signal", "weight": 0.6, "_value": 0.5},
        {"name": "secondary_signal", "weight": 0.4, "_value": 0.5}
    ],
    "scoring": {"aggregation": "weighted_mean"},
    "thresholds": {"concern": 0.20, "notice": 0.40, "healthy": 0.70}
}

score = aggregate(sensor["scoring"]["aggregation"], sensor["signals"])
thresholds = sensor["thresholds"]
band = ("concern" if score <= thresholds["concern"] else
        "notice" if score <= thresholds["notice"] else
        "healthy" if score >= thresholds["healthy"] else "neutral")
print(f"Score: {score:.3f}, Band: {band}")

definitions.definition: — design rule you can use today.
Scale-invariant expansion with even stress distribution.
Chambered growth preserves prior life-support while adding capacity.
Each cycle carries memory inward and extends foresight outward.
Grow by **proportion**, not absolute size; preserve prior chambers.
Migration loops; currents that cross boundaries.
Opposites are phases along one path; separation is a twist, not a wall.
Design flows that traverse both “sides”; avoid binary traps.
Petal arrangements, spiral galaxies, resonant chambers.
Each ring remembers the previous; alignment returns to center.
Keep rings linked to center; repair broken rings to restore resonance.
Minimal surface per volume; force distribution.
Cooperative cells build durable lattice without apex.
Share load across axes; design out single points of failure.

lists: Animal / Image** — approachable doorway.
Geometry** — formal structure.
Physics / Field** — energy behavior.
Ecology / Embodiment** — where it appears in life/land.
Generational Teaching** — long-horizon continuity.
Energy Law / Practice** — design rule you can use today.
Animal**: Nautilus shell.
Geometry**: Logarithmic spiral, golden-ratio scaling.
Physics**: Scale-invariant expansion with even stress distribution.
Ecology**: Chambered growth preserves prior life-support while adding capacity.
Generational**: Each cycle carries memory inward and extends foresight outward.
Energy Law**: Grow by **proportion**, not absolute size; preserve prior chambers.
Animal/Image**: Ouroboros, braided belt, river loop.
Geometry**: Non-orientable surface, single side/edge.
Physics**: Continuity with inversion; phase reversal.
Ecology**: Migration loops; currents that cross boundaries.
Generational**: Opposites are phases along one path; separation is a twist, not a wall.
Energy Law**: Design flows that traverse both “sides”; avoid binary traps.
Image**: Flower, web, galaxy.
Geometry**: Radial symmetry; rings around attractor.
Physics**: Standing waves; resonance fields.
Ecology**: Petal arrangements, spiral galaxies, resonant chambers.
Generational**: Each ring remembers the previous; alignment returns to center.
Energy Law**: Keep rings linked to center; repair broken rings to restore resonance.
Animal**: Bee / hive.
Geometry**: Hexagonal prism; efficient packing.
Physics**: Minimal surface per volume; force distribution.
Ecology**: Honeycomb, basalt columns, snowflake lattices.
Generational**: Cooperative cells build durable lattice without apex.
Energy Law**: Share load across axes; design out single points of failure.
These layers are **science across scopes** (short-term lab ↔ long-term ecosystem).
Use the animal as the door, but **do not stop at the door**. Walk the whole stack.
